description: A 'thasu' person presents ability or achievement without exaggeration or display. The adjective can also qualify a claim whose certainty stays close to its support. 'motu' (humble) concerns inward self-appraisal; a confident person may still describe their work modestly.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th' and let air pass into stressed open central 'a'. Raise the tongue blade close to the alveolar ridge for unvoiced 's', direct air through its central groove, and finish with high-back rounded 'u'.
usage_notes: Placed before a speech or presentation verb, 'thasu' gives the manner. As a quality noun it gives modesty.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A modest claim leaves room for evidence and other speakers without asking anyone to conceal competence.

semantic_domains.communication: ability, achievement, or certainty presented without exaggeration
semantic_domains.community: unshowy self-presentation in relation to other people
